title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover how to save money and avoid crowds during NZ school holidays with our expert travel tips
intro: If you're planning a family trip during NZ school holidays, be prepared for peak prices and crowds at popular destinations like Rotorua and Queenstown. However, with some smart planning, you can save up to 30% on accommodation and activities. Here's how to make the most of your next family adventure.

sections.heading: Understanding Peak and Off-Peak Pricing
sections.body: During school holidays, prices for accommodation, activities, and transportation increase significantly. For example, a family room at the Novotel Rotorua Lakeside can cost around $450 per night during peak season, compared to $280 per night during off-peak season.

sections.heading: Booking Lead Times
list: Book your accommodation at least 3 months in advance to secure the best rates
Reserve popular activities like the Skyline Gondola in Queenstown up to 2 months ahead
Plan your itinerary around off-peak days, such as weekdays, to avoid crowds

sections.heading: Saving Money on Activities
sections.body: Consider purchasing a family pass for popular attractions like the Te Puia thermal reserve in Rotorua, which costs around $120 for a family of four. You can also save money by booking activities online in advance, such as the Shotover Jet in Queenstown, which offers a 10% discount for online bookings.

sections.heading: Getting There
list: Drive times from Auckland to Rotorua: around 3 hours
Drive times from Auckland to Queenstown: around 18 hours (with stops) or 1.5 hours by flight
Book your flights or car rentals at least 2 months in advance to secure the best rates

sections.heading: Plan Your Trip with Our Tools

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: Rent a Campervan for Your Next Adventure
